title: Fix parameters inside std::randomize `with` clause
body:
This PR fixes verilation error when using parameter inside `with` clause.

```
%Error: t_std_randomize.v:128:44: Function/task ref connected to constant instead of variable: 'PARAM'
  128 |         void'(std::randomize(x) with { x > PARAM; });
```

This error is caused by parameter `Var` having its direction set to `VDirection::REF`, because reference cannot be created for `Const` and `VarRef`s to parameters are converted into `Const`s during V3Const. Arguments to std::randomize are converted to `Var`s named `__Varg*` before visiting other arguments in `with` clause. Because of that `captureVariable` method is called only for variables which are not modified by `std::randomize()`, so the direction can be always set to `VDirection::INPUT`.

It also required fixing `ArraySel` comparison, because index was assumed to be `VarRef` but it can also be `Const`. This resulted in a bad argument -> __Varg* conversion which was unnoticeable when the direction was set to `VDirection::REF`.
